skill.name: webapp-testing
skill.description: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.
skill.summary: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying...
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: The static external command, weak crypto, screen upload, and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown examples and ordinary testing language. The skill has no executable scripts and no prompt injection text, but it does instruct browser navigation, screenshots, and console log capture, so users should apply it only to authorized web applications.

medium_findings.title: Browser Testing Can Capture Sensitive Application Data
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally supports testing accessible URLs, capturing screenshots, and reading browser logs. This is legitimate for web testing, but it can expose private data if used on applications without authorization or with sensitive accounts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The capability is explicit and useful, but the file does not direct data exfiltration or unauthorized access. The risk depends on the user's target application and data.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The 13 Ruby or shell backtick findings correspond to JavaScript Markdown code fences and Playwright snippets. They do not execute commands in the skill file and do not create command injection risk by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are inside fenced JavaScript examples or fence delimiters. No shell execution syntax or executable skill script is present.

low_findings.title: Localhost URL Is Legitimate Test Target
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to a local development server used in a Playwright navigation example. It does not send data to an external host.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicitly localhost and appears in a basic navigation test example. No external endpoint or credential transfer is shown.

low_findings.title: Screenshot Upload Finding Is Local Capture Only
low_findings.description: The screenshot examples write images to local paths for debugging. No upload destination, network transfer, or remote storage instruction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code only calls page.screenshot with local file paths. There is no evidence of upload behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm detections on the description and responsive design lines are false positives. The skill contains no cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe web testing and responsive design. No hash, cipher, key, or cryptographic API is present.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is Selector Guidance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance finding points to guidance about choosing web element selectors. It does not request host inventory, credential discovery, or operating system probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line recommends data-testid or role-based web selectors. This is normal UI testing practice and not system reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Frontend bugs are hard to verify from code alone. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Playwright browser checks, screenshots, logs, and user-flow validation.
actual_capabilities: Navigate to local or accessible web application URLs.
Click links, buttons, dropdowns, and other page controls.
Fill forms and verify navigation after submission.
Capture screenshots for visual debugging and documentation.
Inspect browser console logs and network behavior during tests.
Check responsive behavior across different viewport sizes.
limitations: Requires Node.js and a reachable web application target.
Does not test native mobile applications.
Complex authentication flows may need project-specific setup.
Modern frameworks may require additional Playwright configuration.

use_cases.title: Validate a Feature Before Release
use_cases.description: Run browser checks against a local feature branch and confirm expected UI behavior.

use_cases.title: Debug a Broken User Flow
use_cases.description: Reproduce a failing path, inspect logs, and capture screenshots that show the failure.

use_cases.title: Review Responsive Layout Changes
use_cases.description: Open the same page at multiple viewport sizes and verify key elements remain usable.

prompt_templates.title: Open a Local Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for a running app
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the webapp-testing skill to open my local app at http://localhost:3000 and confirm the page title and main heading.

prompt_templates.title: Test a Form Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the webapp-testing skill to fill the sign-in form, submit it, and report whether the dashboard page loads correctly.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Console Errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the webapp-testing skill to reproduce the issue on the settings page, collect console errors, and capture a screenshot of the failed state.

prompt_templates.title: Verify Responsive Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the webapp-testing skill to test the checkout flow on desktop, tablet, and mobile viewports. Summarize layout problems and interaction failures.

output_examples.input: Check whether the homepage loads and shows the expected navigation.
output_examples.output: The homepage loaded successfully. The title, navigation links, and primary call-to-action were visible. No console errors appeared during load.

output_examples.input: Test the account form and capture evidence if it fails.
output_examples.output: The form accepted the entered values, but submission did not navigate to the account page. A screenshot was saved for debugging.

output_examples.input: Review the pricing page on mobile and desktop.
output_examples.output: Desktop layout was usable. On mobile, the plan comparison table overflowed horizontally and the upgrade button was partly hidden.

best_practices: Test only applications and accounts you are authorized to access.
Use stable selectors such as roles or data-testid attributes.
Capture screenshots and console logs when behavior differs from expectations.
anti_patterns: Do not use it against third-party sites without explicit permission.
Do not rely only on screenshots when assertions can verify behavior.
Do not store screenshots that contain secrets or private user data.

faq.question: Does this skill install Playwright?
faq.answer: The skill says Playwright can be installed automatically if it is not present. Confirm package installation before running it.

faq.question: Can it test production websites?
faq.answer: It can open accessible URLs, but production testing should be authorized, limited, and handled with care.

faq.question: Can it capture screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes screenshot capture for debugging and failed test evidence.

faq.question: Does it inspect console logs?
faq.answer: Yes. It can collect browser console messages to help diagnose frontend errors.

faq.question: Can it test mobile apps?
faq.answer: No. The skill is for web applications in a browser, not native mobile applications.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the target URL, expected behavior, test account details if needed, and any flows you want verified.
